system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "system";
    object      controlDict;
}

application     pimpleFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         0.5;

deltaT          0.0025;

writeControl    runTime;

writeInterval   0.05;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.01;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
    }

    pFinal
    {
        $p;
        relTol          0;
    }

    U
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.01;
    }

    UFinal
    {
        $U;
        relTol          0;
    }
}

PIMPLE
{
    nOuterCorrectors    2;
    nCorrectors         1;
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='momentumTransport', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      momentumTransport;
}

simulationType  laminar;

laminar
{
    laminarModel    generalizedNewtonian;

    generalizedNewtonianCoeffs
    {
        viscosityModel  CrossPowerLaw;

        nuInf           10;
        m               0.4;
        n               3;
    }
}'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='physicalProperties', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "constant";
    object      physicalProperties;
}

viscosityModel  constant;

nu              0.01;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='U', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;

    object      U;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

dimensions      [0 1 -1 0 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform (0 0 0);

boundaryField
{
    inlet
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform (1 0 0);
    }

    outlet
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }

    top
    {
        type            noSlip;
    }

    bottom
    {
        type            noSlip;
    }

    cylinder
    {
        type            noSlip;
    }

    defaultFaces
    {
        type            empty;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='p', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile

internalField   uniform 0;

boundaryField
{

    {

    }

    top
    {

    }

    {

    }

    {

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='blockMeshDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      blockMeshDict;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

scale   1;

vertices
(
    // Bottom row (y = -0.5)
    (-1 -0.5 -0.5)    // 0
    (-0.2 -0.5 -0.5)  // 1
    ( 1 -0.5 -0.5)    // 2
    (-1 -0.5  0.5)    // 3
    (-0.2 -0.5  0.5)  // 4
    ( 1 -0.5  0.5)    // 5

    // Top row (y = 0.5)
    (-0.2  0.5 -0.5)  // 6
    ( 1  0.5 -0.5)    // 7
    (-1  0.5 -0.5)    // 8
    (-0.2  0.5  0.5)  // 9
    ( 1  0.5  0.5)    // 10
    (-1  0.5  0.5)    // 11

    // Arc intermediate points (y = 0 plane)
    (0 -0.2 0)        // 12
    (0.3  0 -0.5)     // 13
    (0.3  0  0.5)     // 14
    (-0.3  1.5 -0.5)  // 15
    (-0.3  1.5  0.5)  // 16
);

blocks
(
    // Block 0: bottom section (combined originally blocks 0 and 1)
    hex (0 2 5 3 12 13 14 11) (90 15 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 1: top section (combined originally blocks 2 and 3)
    hex (8 11 15 9 12 13 16 14) (90 15 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);

edges
(
    arc 12 13 (0 -0.2 0);
    arc 13 14 (0.3 0 -0.5);
    arc 14 15 (0.3 0 0.5);
    arc 15 16 (-0.3 1.5 -0.5);
);

boundary
(
    inlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 3 11 2)
            (8 11 15 9)
        );
    }
    outlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (2 5 14 7)
            (14 16 15 10)
        );
    }
    top
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (-0.3 1.5 -0.5) (-0.3 1.5 0.5) (-1 1.5 -0.5) (-1 1.5 0.5)
        );
    }
    bottom
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (0 2 13 12)
            (12 13 14 11)
        );
    }
    cylinder
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (1 4 9 6)
            (6 9 15 11)
        );
    }
    frontAndBack
    {
        type empty;
        faces
        (
            (0 2 11 3)
            (12 13 14 11)
            (8 11 15 9)
            (12 13 16 14)
        );
    }
);

mergePatchPairs
(
);

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         Euler;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(p)         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         Gauss linear;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div((nuEff*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

// ************************************************************************* //')]</foamfiles>

<reviewer_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Error 1: `log.blockMesh` — Arc Point Collinear with Endpoints

**Error Message:**
```
Arc point (0 -0.2 0) lies on the line of edge (12 13)
```

**Root Cause:**

The error states that the arc point `(0 -0.2 0)` (vertex 12) lies on the line of edge `(12 13)`. This means the three points defining the arc are collinear. Looking at the current `blockMeshDict`:

- Vertex 12: `(0 -0.2 0)`
- Vertex 13: `(0.3 0 -0.5)`
- Vertex 14: `(0.3 0 0.5)`

The issue is specifically with **edge 12→13**. The arc from vertex 12 to vertex 13 uses vertex 12 itself as the intermediate point, which is degenerate — the arc starts at vertex 12 and the "midpoint" is also vertex 12. This is fundamentally flawed.

Looking at the `arc` edge definitions:
```
arc 12 13 (0 -0.2 0);
arc 13 14 (0.3 0 -0.5);
arc 14 15 (0.3 0 0.5);
arc 15 16 (-0.3 1.5 -0.5);
```

Each `arc` command has only **one** intermediate point listed in parentheses, but the syntax requires **at least two** points to define a meaningful arc curve. With only one point, that point becomes the starting vertex, causing the collinearity error.

**Proposed Fix:**

In OpenFOAM's `blockMeshDict`, an `arc` edge requires **at least two intermediate points** (three total points defining the curve). Update the `arc` edge definitions to include proper intermediate points:

```
arc 12 13 (0 -0.2 0) (0.3 0 -0.5);
arc 13 14 (0.3 0 -0.5) (0.3 0 0.5);
arc 14 15 (0.3 0 0.5) (-0.3 1.5 -0.5);
arc 15 16 (-0.3 1.5 -0.5) (-0.3 1.5 0.5);
```

This provides two distinct points per arc, forming a proper curve. The first arc goes from vertex 12 to vertex 13 via the two intermediate points (0,-0.2,0) and (0.3,0,-0.5). Similarly for the others.

## Error 2: `log.pimpleFoam` — Cascading Failure

Since `blockMesh` fails, no mesh exists. This resolves automatically once `blockMesh` succeeds.

## Summary

**Only fix needed:** Modify the `arc` edge definitions in `system/blockMeshDict` to include **two intermediate points** per arc instead of one. The current file has one-point arcs which cause degeneracy. Add a second point to each `arc` command as shown above. All other files (boundary conditions, transport models, solvers, schemes) are correct and require no changes.</reviewer_analysis>
<rewrite_plan>{'target_files': [{'file': 'system/blockMeshDict', 'changes': 'Change arc 12 13 from single point (0 -0.2 0) to two points (0 -0.2 0) (0.3 0 -0.5); Change arc 13 14 from single point (0.3 0 -0.5) to two points (0.3 0 -0.5) (0.3 0 0.5); Change arc 14 15 from single point (0.3 0 0.5) to two points (0.3 0 0.5) (-0.3 1.5 -0.5); Change arc 15 16 from single point (-0.3 1.5 -0.5) to two points (-0.3 1.5 -0.5) (-0.3 1.5 0.5)'}]}</rewrite_plan>

<user_requirement>do a laminar offsetcylinder simulation. Use PIMPLE algorithm with a generalized Newtonian model and Cross-Power-Law viscosity for momentum transport, characterized by specific parameters nuInf=10, m=0.4, n=3. Boundary conditions specify a fixed velocity of 1m/s at the inlet (left),zero gradient pressure at the outlet (right), and no-slip conditions for walls, including the cylinder surface. Use timestep of 0.0025 and output every 0.05. Finaltime is 0.5. use constant viscosity model with nu value of 0.01 in physical properties.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
